SEARCH

Class Lab05Tests

java.lang.Object
Lab05Tests
public class Lab05Tests extends Object
Unit tests for Word and WordList. Structure: - Word tests: constructor, increment, toString, compareTo. - WordList Lab 4 tests: construction, filtering, search, getMostFrequent, topKMostFrequent. - WordList Lab 5 tests: distinctWordsCount, shortWordsCount (including assertThrows for negative threshold), mostFrequentByLetter. + there are some explanations to some tests(needed for myself)
Version:
3/3/26
Author:
Ali Kablanbek

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Lab05Tests()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    testAllStopWords()
    WHY: All stop-words input must produce an empty (not null) list.
    void
    testCompareToEqualFrequency()
    WHY: Two objects with equal frequency must return 0.
    void
    testCompareToExactDifference()
    WHY: The lab spec says compareTo equals this.frequency - w.frequency exactly.
    void
    testCompareToGreater()
    WHY: Higher-frequency caller must return a positive value.
    void
    testCompareToLess()
    WHY: Lower-frequency caller must return a negative value.
    void
    testCompareToSelf()
    WHY: The lab demos self-comparison returning 0 (n - n == 0).
    void
    testDistinctWordsCountAllSame()
    WHYYYY: All identical tokens must return 1, not the raw count.
    void
    testDistinctWordsCountEmptyArray()
    WHY: Empty array must return 0 without throwing.
    void
    testDistinctWordsCountIgnoresEmptyStrings()
    WHY?: split() can produce empty strings at array boundaries; they must not be counted as distinct words.
    void
    testDistinctWordsCountNormal()
    WHY: Repeated tokens must be collapsed; 5 raw tokens → 3 distinct strings.
    void
    testEmptyTokenArray()
    WHY: Empty token array must produce an empty list without throwing.
    void
    testFrequencyCounting()
    WHY: Each unique non-stop token must accumulate the correct count.
    void
    testGetMostFrequent()
    WHY: getMostFrequent() must identify the word with the highest count.
    void
    testGetMostFrequentAllTied()
    WHY: In a tie, getMostFrequent() must still return a valid Word.
    void
    testGetMostFrequentEmptyList()
    WHY: getMostFrequent() must return null for an empty list, not throw.
    void
    testGetWordFrequency()
    WHY: getWordFrequency() must return a non-null list of the correct size.
    void
    testIncrementMultipleTimes()
    WHY: Multiple increments must accumulate, not reset or cap.
    void
    testIncrementOnce()
    WHY: One increment is the most common case; counter must go from 1 to 2.
    void
    testMostFrequentByLetterCaseSensitive()
    WHY: Tokens are lowercased in main, so uppercase 'P' should find nothing in a properly built WordList.
    void
    testMostFrequentByLetterNoMatch()
    WHY: No matching letter means the filtered stream is empty; orElse(null) must return null rather than throw.
    void
    testMostFrequentByLetterNormal()
    WhY: "python"(4) beats "prime"(2) among 'p'-words; "castle"(3) is excluded by the filter even though it has higher frequency than "prime".
    void
    testMostFrequentByLetterSingleMatch()
    WHY: max() on a single-element stream must return that element.
    void
    testSearchNotFound()
    WHY: search() must return -1 for an absent word.
    void
    testSearchReturnsCorrectIndex()
    WHY: The returned index must point to the correct Word, not just be >= 0.
    void
    testShortWordsCountNegativeThrows()
    WHY: A negative threshold has no meaningful definition and must throw IllegalArgumentException.
    void
    testShortWordsCountNegativeThrowsMessage()
    WHY: assertThrows returns the caught exception so we can inspect its message and confirm the right code path was hit.
    void
    testShortWordsCountNormal()
    WHY: With threshold 3, "cat"(3) and "ox"(2) qualify; "lion"(4) does not.
    void
    testShortWordsCountThresholdCoversAll()
    WHY: Threshold >= longest word means every word qualifies.
    void
    testShortWordsCountThresholdZero()
    WHY: Threshold 0 means no word can qualify (every string has length >= 1).
    void
    testStopWordsCaseInsensitive()
    WHY: stopwords.txt has mixed-case entries like "But", so isStopWord() must use equalsIgnoreCase() to catch them after toLowerCase().
    void
    testStopWordsFiltered()
    WHY: Stop words must be silently excluded from wordFrequency.
    void
    testTopKExceedsListSize()
    WHY: k > list size must cap at list size with no null entries.
    void
    testTopKMostFrequent()
    WHY: Verifies correct descending order and array length.
    void
    testTopOneMostFrequent()
    WHY: topKMostFrequent(1) and getMostFrequent() must agree.
    void
    testToStringAfterIncrement()
    WHY: toString() must reflect the current (live) frequency, not a snapshot.
    void
    testToStringFormat()
    WHY: The lab specifies the exact format invalid input: '<'word, frequency>.
    void
    testWordConstructorAndGetters()
    WHY: Confirms the constructor stores the word and starts frequency at 1.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Lab05Tests

      public Lab05Tests()

  • Method Details

    • testWordConstructorAndGetters

      @Test public void testWordConstructorAndGetters()
      WHY: Confirms the constructor stores the word and starts frequency at 1.

    • testIncrementOnce

      @Test public void testIncrementOnce()
      WHY: One increment is the most common case; counter must go from 1 to 2.

    • testIncrementMultipleTimes

      @Test public void testIncrementMultipleTimes()
      WHY: Multiple increments must accumulate, not reset or cap.

    • testToStringFormat

      @Test public void testToStringFormat()
      WHY: The lab specifies the exact format invalid input: '<'word, frequency>.

    • testToStringAfterIncrement

      @Test public void testToStringAfterIncrement()
      WHY: toString() must reflect the current (live) frequency, not a snapshot.

    • testCompareToSelf

      @Test public void testCompareToSelf()
      WHY: The lab demos self-comparison returning 0 (n - n == 0).

    • testCompareToEqualFrequency

      @Test public void testCompareToEqualFrequency()
      WHY: Two objects with equal frequency must return 0.

    • testCompareToLess

      @Test public void testCompareToLess()
      WHY: Lower-frequency caller must return a negative value.

    • testCompareToGreater

      @Test public void testCompareToGreater()
      WHY: Higher-frequency caller must return a positive value.

    • testCompareToExactDifference

      @Test public void testCompareToExactDifference()
      WHY: The lab spec says compareTo equals this.frequency - w.frequency exactly.

    • testStopWordsFiltered

      @Test public void testStopWordsFiltered()
      WHY: Stop words must be silently excluded from wordFrequency.

    • testStopWordsCaseInsensitive

      @Test public void testStopWordsCaseInsensitive()
      WHY: stopwords.txt has mixed-case entries like "But", so isStopWord() must use equalsIgnoreCase() to catch them after toLowerCase().

    • testFrequencyCounting

      @Test public void testFrequencyCounting()
      WHY: Each unique non-stop token must accumulate the correct count.

    • testAllStopWords

      @Test public void testAllStopWords()
      WHY: All stop-words input must produce an empty (not null) list.

    • testEmptyTokenArray

      @Test public void testEmptyTokenArray()
      WHY: Empty token array must produce an empty list without throwing.

    • testGetWordFrequency

      @Test public void testGetWordFrequency()
      WHY: getWordFrequency() must return a non-null list of the correct size.

    • testSearchNotFound

      @Test public void testSearchNotFound()
      WHY: search() must return -1 for an absent word.

    • testSearchReturnsCorrectIndex

      @Test public void testSearchReturnsCorrectIndex()
      WHY: The returned index must point to the correct Word, not just be >= 0.

    • testGetMostFrequent

      @Test public void testGetMostFrequent()
      WHY: getMostFrequent() must identify the word with the highest count.

    • testGetMostFrequentEmptyList

      @Test public void testGetMostFrequentEmptyList()
      WHY: getMostFrequent() must return null for an empty list, not throw.

    • testGetMostFrequentAllTied

      @Test public void testGetMostFrequentAllTied()
      WHY: In a tie, getMostFrequent() must still return a valid Word.

    • testTopKMostFrequent

      @Test public void testTopKMostFrequent()
      WHY: Verifies correct descending order and array length.

    • testTopKExceedsListSize

      @Test public void testTopKExceedsListSize()
      WHY: k > list size must cap at list size with no null entries.

    • testTopOneMostFrequent

      @Test public void testTopOneMostFrequent()
      WHY: topKMostFrequent(1) and getMostFrequent() must agree.

    • testDistinctWordsCountNormal

      @Test public void testDistinctWordsCountNormal()
      WHY: Repeated tokens must be collapsed; 5 raw tokens → 3 distinct strings.

    • testDistinctWordsCountAllSame

      @Test public void testDistinctWordsCountAllSame()
      WHYYYY: All identical tokens must return 1, not the raw count.

    • testDistinctWordsCountIgnoresEmptyStrings

      @Test public void testDistinctWordsCountIgnoresEmptyStrings()
      WHY?: split() can produce empty strings at array boundaries; they must not be counted as distinct words.

    • testDistinctWordsCountEmptyArray

      @Test public void testDistinctWordsCountEmptyArray()
      WHY: Empty array must return 0 without throwing.

    • testShortWordsCountNormal

      @Test public void testShortWordsCountNormal()
      WHY: With threshold 3, "cat"(3) and "ox"(2) qualify; "lion"(4) does not. Verifies the filter is invalid input: '<'= (inclusive), not invalid input: '<'.

    • testShortWordsCountThresholdZero

      @Test public void testShortWordsCountThresholdZero()
      WHY: Threshold 0 means no word can qualify (every string has length >= 1).

    • testShortWordsCountThresholdCoversAll

      @Test public void testShortWordsCountThresholdCoversAll()
      WHY: Threshold >= longest word means every word qualifies.

    • testShortWordsCountNegativeThrows

      @Test public void testShortWordsCountNegativeThrows()
      WHY: A negative threshold has no meaningful definition and must throw IllegalArgumentException. The lambda () -> ... is the code under test; assertThrows catches the exception and fails the test if none is thrown.

    • testShortWordsCountNegativeThrowsMessage

      @Test public void testShortWordsCountNegativeThrowsMessage()
      WHY: assertThrows returns the caught exception so we can inspect its message and confirm the right code path was hit.

    • testMostFrequentByLetterNormal

      @Test public void testMostFrequentByLetterNormal()
      WhY: "python"(4) beats "prime"(2) among 'p'-words; "castle"(3) is excluded by the filter even though it has higher frequency than "prime".

    • testMostFrequentByLetterNoMatch

      @Test public void testMostFrequentByLetterNoMatch()
      WHY: No matching letter means the filtered stream is empty; orElse(null) must return null rather than throw.

    • testMostFrequentByLetterSingleMatch

      @Test public void testMostFrequentByLetterSingleMatch()
      WHY: max() on a single-element stream must return that element.

    • testMostFrequentByLetterCaseSensitive

      @Test public void testMostFrequentByLetterCaseSensitive()
      WHY: Tokens are lowercased in main, so uppercase 'P' should find nothing in a properly built WordList. Confirms the method is case-sensitive.
